---
title: "Programming with LLMs"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_large-language-models_m4no.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 45%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Hello!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

## Day 1: Build on AI

1.  Talking with LLMs via code 
2.  Programming with LLMs (this session!)
3.  Prompt engineering and RAG
4.  Beyond prompts

## Build your own Shiny chat app

-   Shiny `chat_ui()` and `chat_append()` 
-   Shiny for R also has `chat_mod_server()`

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

-   Open `04-word-game-reverse`
-   Flip the word game from the last session so:
    - the LLM holds a secret word and gives hints
    - the user has to guess
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}


# Choosing a model {background-image="../images/undraw_large-language-models_m4no.svg" background-size="auto 45%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

## The landscape

-   Major providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, plus local options like ollama
-   What are the tradeoffs? 
    -   Capability, context length, speed, cost
-   Newer is not always better for your task

## Comparing providers

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Demo

The same prompt across several providers: compare answers, latency, and token usage.

```r
# demo/02-models/02-models.qmd
chat("anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6")$chat(prompt)
chat("openai/gpt-5")$chat(prompt)
```

:::

# Multi-modal input {background-image="../images/undraw_large-language-models_m4no.svg" background-size="auto 45%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

## Beyond text

-   Vision 👀
    - Pass an image alongside your prompt
-   PDFs 📄
    - Pass document bytes directly
-   When to extract text up front vs. let the model see the file?

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Try multi-modal input:

-   Open `05-vision` and pass food photos to ask for recipe suggestions
-   Open `06-pdf` and take a PDF recipe and turn it into clean Markdown
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

# Structured output {background-image="../images/undraw_large-language-models_m4no.svg" background-size="auto 45%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

## Getting parseable answers

-   R: `ellmer::type_*()`
-   Python: Pydantic models with `chatlas`
-   Schemas double as documentation for the model

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

- Open `07-structured-output` and extract structured fields from the recipe PDF:
    - Ingredients, steps, yield, prep time
- For Python, turn on `use_attribute_docstrings` in Pydantic to let docstrings guide the model
:::

{{< countdown minutes=15 >}}

# Parallel and batch calls {background-image="../images/undraw_large-language-models_m4no.svg" background-size="auto 45%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

## Scaling out

-   When one prompt becomes a hundred
-   Parallel API calls vs. provider batch APIs (cheaper, slower)
-   Watch for rate limits!

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

- Open `08-batch` and re-run the structured extraction across many recipes in parallel
- Save the JSON output so we can reuse it later
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/)
:::
